brief_justification: Correct answer (No) with valid reasoning; Deep analysis referencing Knobe Effect asymmetry and tracing second-order implications of intentionality judgments; Some self-monitoring via suppress signal application but no explicit bias-checking of own reasoning process; Basic verification by checking answer against the board's stated words, but no counterfactual or boundary testing; Acknowledges the asymmetry phenomenon but doesn't explicitly state confidence levels or assumptions; Systematically contrasts the positive vs negative side-effect versions and explains why people judge them differently; Full derivation chain from board's words to intention analysis to philosophical framework, fully reproducible.
